Source: Bureau of Soils & Water Management (BSWM) / FAO Soil Interpretation Guidelines
==========================================================================================
Crop     : Coffee
Soil Type: Unknown
==========================================================================================

1. SOIL CONDITION ASSESSMENT
+----------------------+------------+-----------+------------------------------------------------------+
| Parameter            | Value      | Rating    | Remarks                                              |
+----------------------+------------+-----------+------------------------------------------------------+
| Soil pH              | N/A        | N/A       | No data.                                             |
| Organic Matter       | N/A        | N/A       | No data.                                             |
| Nitrogen (N)         | N/A        | N/A       | No data.                                             |
| Phosphorus (P)       | N/A        | N/A       | No data.                                             |
| Potassium (K)        | N/A        | N/A       | No data.                                             |
+----------------------+------------+-----------+------------------------------------------------------+

2. FERTILIZER RECOMMENDATION
   (Note: full application timing is in Section 4 below)
+-----------------+--------------------------------------+--------------------+----------------------+
| Nutrient        | Fertilizer Product                   | Rate (per ha)      | When to Apply        |
+-----------------+--------------------------------------+--------------------+----------------------+
| Nitrogen (N)    | N/A                                  | N/A                | N/A                  |
| Phosphorus (P)  | N/A                                  | N/A                | N/A                  |
| Potassium (K)   | N/A                                  | N/A                | N/A                  |
+-----------------+--------------------------------------+--------------------+----------------------+

3. SOIL AMENDMENT
+------------------------+------------------------------------+--------------+--------------------------+
| Concern                | Product                            | Rate         | When to Apply            |
+------------------------+------------------------------------+--------------+--------------------------+
| None needed            | Soil pH and OM are at good levels. | -            | Continue current managem |
+------------------------+------------------------------------+--------------+--------------------------+

4. APPLICATION SCHEDULE SUMMARY
+----------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| When                       | Action / What to Do                                                  |
+----------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------+
| Every planting season      | Apply maintenance fertilizer based on crop needs.                    |
| Every year                 | Apply organic matter (compost/manure) to maintain soil health.       |
| Every 6-12 months          | Re-test soil to evaluate progress and update fertilizer plan.        |
+----------------------------+----------------------------------------------------------------------+

5. GOOD FARMING PRACTICES
  * Mulch around coffee plants with dried leaves or rice straw (5-10 cm thick).
  * Keep crop residues on the field after harvest -- do NOT burn them.
  * Plant legume cover crops during fallow season to naturally add nitrogen.
  * Avoid deep plowing to protect soil structure and organic matter.
  * Never apply fertilizer on extremely dry or waterlogged soil.
  * Always wash hands after handling fertilizers and chemicals.

==========================================================================================
IMPORTANT REMINDERS
==========================================================================================
  ! ALWAYS correct soil pH FIRST -- fertilizers do not work on wrong pH.
  ! SPLIT nitrogen and potassium -- never apply the full dose at once.
  ! RE-TEST your soil every 6-12 months after major amendments.
  ! BANDED application of phosphorus near roots is more efficient than broadcasting.

databaseQueries.query: SELECT `permissions`.*, `model_has_permissions`.`model_id` as `pivot_model_id`, `model_has_permissions`.`permission_id` as `pivot_permission_id`, `model_has_permissions`.`model_type` as `pivot_model_type` FROM `permissions` inner join `model_has_permissions` on `permissions`.`id` = `model_has_permissions`.`permission_id` WHERE `model_has_permissions`.`model_id` in (4) and `model_has_permissions`.`model_type` = 'App\\Models\\User'

SELECT `roles`.*, `model_has_roles`.`model_id` as `pivot_model_id`, `model_has_roles`.`role_id` as `pivot_role_id`, `model_has_roles`.`model_type` as `pivot_model_type` FROM `roles` inner join `model_has_roles` on `roles`.`id` = `model_has_roles`.`role_id` WHERE `model_has_roles`.`model_id` in (4) and `model_has_roles`.`model_type` = 'App\\Models\\User'
SELECT * FROM `agricultural_professionals` WHERE `agricultural_professionals`.`user_id` = 4 and `agricultural_professionals`.`user_id` IS not NULL LIMIT 1
SELECT * FROM `soil_analysis` ORDER BY `id` DESC LIMIT 10 offset 0
SELECT `farms`.`farm_name`, `farms`.`id` FROM `farms` ORDER BY `farms`.`farm_name` ASC LIMIT 50
